𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twenty-Three — Part Two

The Book of Nekhemyah

NEKHEMYAH

Covenant History — The Rebuilding of the Walls

Chapters 1 through 13

I Am Doing a Great Work and Cannot Come Down — The Joy of YHWH Is Your Strength — The Joy of Yerushalayim Was Heard Far Away — Remember Me O My Creator for Good

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

Orit Ge'ez — Ethiopian Complete Canon

Masoretic Text

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

NEKHEMYAH — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 7 — THE COVENANT PRAYER AND THE COVENANT WALLS

NEKHEMYAH — CHAPTER 1 The Words of Nekhemyah — The Survivors Are in Great Trouble — The Wall Is Broken Down — I Sat Down and Wept — I Prayed Before the Creator — Remember the Word You Commanded Mosheh

1 The words of Nekhemyah the son of Hakhalyahu. Now it happened in the month of Kislev, in the twentieth year, as I was in Shushan the citadel,

2 That Hanani, one of my brothers, came with certain men from Yehudah. And I asked them concerning the Yehudim who escaped, who had survived the exile, and concerning Yerushalayim.

3 And they said to me, The survivors there in the province who escaped exile are in great trouble and shame. The wall of Yerushalayim is broken down, and its gates are destroyed by fire.

4 As soon as I heard these words I sat down and wept and mourned for days, and I continued fasting and praying before the Creator of heaven.

5 And I said, O YHWH Creator of heaven, the great and awesome Creator who keeps covenant and steadfast love with those who love him and keep his commandments,

6 Let your ear be attentive and your eyes open, to hear the prayer of your servant that I now pray before you day and night for the people of Yisra'EL your servants, confessing the sins of the people of Yisra'EL, which we have sinned against you. Even I and my father's house have sinned.

7 We have acted very corruptly against you and have not kept the commandments, the statutes, and the rules that you commanded your servant Mosheh.

8 Remember the word that you commanded your servant Mosheh, saying, If you are unfaithful, I will scatter you among the peoples,

9 But if you return to me and keep my commandments and do them, though your outcasts are in the uttermost parts of heaven, from there I will gather them and bring them to the place that I have chosen, to make my name dwell there.

10 They are your servants and your people, whom you have redeemed by your great power and by your strong hand.

11 O Creator, let your ear be attentive to the prayer of your servant, and to the prayer of your servants who delight to fear your name, and give success to your servant today, and grant him mercy in the sight of this man. Now I was cupbearer to the king.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No substantial Nekhemyah text was found among the Dead Sea Scrolls. The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic traditions are the primary authorities. Nekhemyah's prayer of Chapter 1 is the covenant model of diaspora covenant intercession: identification with the covenant community's covenant sin, appeal to the covenant promises given through Mosheh, and specific covenant petition for covenant practical covenant access to the covenant king. The covenant prayer is both covenant theological and covenant pragmatically purposeful.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full. Verse 5-9 — the great and awesome Creator who keeps covenant and steadfast love — we have sinned — remember the word you commanded Mosheh — from there I will gather them. Nekhemyah's covenant prayer draws directly on Devarim 30:1-5: the covenant scattering and the covenant regathering are both covenant promised in the covenant Torah. Nekhemyah prays the covenant Torah back to YHWH as covenant ground for covenant present covenant action.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 1 in full. Verse 11 — now I was cupbearer to the king. The covenant final verse of the covenant prayer identifies Nekhemyah's covenant position: the cupbearer to the king of Persia. The covenant proximity to covenant power that will be the covenant instrument of the covenant rebuilding of Yerushalayim's walls is covenant identified at the covenant moment of covenant prayer. Covenant prayer and covenant providential covenant position are covenant simultaneous.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4 — I sat down and wept and mourned for days. Nekhemyah's covenant response to the news of Yerushalayim's covenant condition is covenant immediate and covenant sustained: not quick covenant sympathy but days of covenant weeping, covenant mourning, covenant fasting, and covenant prayer. The covenant leader who will eventually rebuild the walls begins by sitting down and weeping over them. Covenant action is preceded by covenant genuine covenant grief.

NEKHEMYAH — CHAPTER 2 The King Asked Why Is Your Face Sad — I Prayed to the Creator — Send Me to Yehudah — The King Granted Me — I Inspected the Walls by Night — They Laughed at Us — The Creator of Heaven Will Make Us Prosper

1 In the month of Nisan, in the twentieth year of Artakhshasta the king, when wine was before him, I took up the wine and gave it to the king. Now I had not been sad in his presence.

2 And the king said to me, Why is your face sad, seeing you are not sick? This is nothing but sadness of the heart. Then I was very much afraid.

3 I said to the king, Let the king live forever. Why should not my face be sad, when the city, the place of my fathers' graves, lies in ruins, and its gates have been destroyed by fire?

4 Then the king said to me, What are you requesting? So I prayed to the Creator of heaven.

5 And I said to the king, If it pleases the king, and if your servant has found favor in your sight, that you send me to Yehudah, to the city of my fathers' graves, that I may rebuild it.

6 And the king said to me, with the queen sitting beside him, How long will you be gone, and when will you return? So it pleased the king to send me when I had given him a time.

7 And I said to the king, If it pleases the king, let letters be given me to the governors of the province Beyond the River, that they may let me pass through until I come to Yehudah,

8 And a letter to Asaf, the keeper of the king's forest, that he may give me timber to make beams for the gates of the fortress of the temple, and for the wall of the city, and for the house that I shall occupy. And the king granted me what I asked, for the good hand of my Creator was upon me.

9 Then I came to the governors of the province Beyond the River and gave them the king's letters. Now the king had sent with me officers of the army and horsemen.

10 But when Sanballat the Horonite and Toviyahu the Ammonite servant heard this, it displeased them greatly that someone had come to seek the welfare of the people of Yisra'EL.

11 So I went to Yerushalayim and was there three days.

12 Then I arose in the night, I and a few men with me. And I told no one what my Creator had put into my heart to do for Yerushalayim. There was no animal with me but the one on which I rode.

13 I went out by night by the Valley Gate to the Dragon Spring and to the Dung Gate, and I inspected the walls of Yerushalayim that were broken down and its gates that had been destroyed by fire.

14 Then I went on to the Fountain Gate and to the King's Pool, but there was no room for the animal that was under me to pass.

15 Then I went up in the night by the valley and inspected the wall, and I turned back and entered by the Valley Gate, and so returned.

16 And the officials did not know where I had gone or what I was doing, and I had not yet told the Yehudim, the priests, the nobles, the officials, and the rest who were to do the work.

17 Then I said to them, You see the trouble we are in, how Yerushalayim lies in ruins with its gates burned. Come, let us build the wall of Yerushalayim, that we may no longer suffer derision.

18 And I told them of the hand of my Creator that had been upon me for good, and also of the words that the king had spoken to me. And they said, Let us rise up and build. So they strengthened their hands for the good work.

19 But when Sanballat the Horonite and Toviyahu the Ammonite servant and Geshem the Arab heard of it, they jeered at us and despised us and said, What is this thing that you are doing? Are you rebelling against the king?

20 Then I replied to them, The Creator of heaven will make us prosper, and we his servants will arise and build, but you have no portion or right or claim in Yerushalayim.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant night survey of Yerushalayim's walls — verses 12-16 — is the covenant intelligence-gathering that precedes the covenant public covenant mobilization. Nekhemyah covenant inspects the covenant problem in covenant private before announcing it covenant publicly. The covenant leader who does not fully understand the covenant situation cannot covenant mobilize effectively. Covenant reconnaissance precedes covenant revelation precedes covenant response.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 4 — the king said, What are you requesting? So I prayed to the Creator of heaven. The covenant instantaneous prayer: between the covenant king's question and Nekhemyah's covenant answer is a covenant silent prayer. The covenant cupbearer does not answer until he has first addressed the covenant Creator. The covenant prayer is the covenant hinge between the covenant problem and the covenant response.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 20 — The Creator of heaven will make us prosper, and we his servants will arise and build, but you have no portion or right or claim in Yerushalayim. The covenant boundary-setting: the covenant adversaries who mock the covenant work are excluded from covenant participation in the covenant community and the covenant city. The covenant project belongs to covenant servants of YHWH — not to those who oppose it.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17-18 — Nekhemyah's covenant mobilization speech is two sentences: you see the trouble — come let us build. And the covenant reason: I told them of the hand of my Creator that had been upon me, and the words of the king. The covenant mobilization combines covenant theological covenant grounds — YHWH's covenant hand — and covenant practical covenant authorization — the king's covenant words. Covenant faith and covenant practical covenant permission together produce covenant community covenant action.

NEKHEMYAH — CHAPTER 3 Elyashiv the High Priest Rose — They Built the Sheep Gate — The Sons of Sena'ah Built the Fish Gate — Section by Section Each One Repaired — Opposite His Own House

1 Then Elyashiv the high priest rose up with his brothers the priests, and they built the Sheep Gate. They consecrated it and set its doors. They consecrated it as far as the Tower of the Hundred, as far as the Tower of Hanan'el.

2 And next to him the men of Yericho built. And next to them Zakkur the son of Imri built.

3 The sons of Sena'ah built the Fish Gate. They laid its beams and set its doors, its bolts, and its bars.

4 And next to them Meremot the son of Uriyahu, son of Hakkoz repaired. And next to them Meshullam the son of Berekhyahu, son of Meshezavel repaired. And next to them Tzadok the son of Ba'ana repaired.

5 And next to them the Tekoites repaired, but their nobles would not stoop to serve their lord.

6 Yo'iada the son of Paseakh and Meshullam the son of Besodeyahu repaired the Gate of Yeshanah. They laid its beams and set its doors, its bolts, and its bars.

7 And next to them repaired Melatyahu the Gibeonite and Yadon the Meronothite, the men of Givon and of Mitzpah, the seat of the governor of the province Beyond the River.

8 Next to him Uzziel the son of Harhayahu, goldsmiths, repaired. Next to him Hananyahu, one of the perfumers, repaired, and they restored Yerushalayim as far as the Broad Wall.

9 Next to them Refayahu the son of Hur, ruler of half the district of Yerushalayim, repaired.

10 Next to them Yedayahu the son of Harumaf repaired opposite his house. And next to him Hattush the son of Hashabneyahu repaired.

11 Malkiyahu the son of Harim and Hashuv the son of Pakhath-Moav repaired another section and the Tower of the Ovens.

12 Next to him Shallum the son of Hallohesh, ruler of half the district of Yerushalayim, repaired, he and his daughters.

13 Hanun and the inhabitants of Zanoah repaired the Valley Gate. They rebuilt it and set its doors, its bolts, and its bars, and repaired 1,000 cubits of the wall, as far as the Dung Gate.

14 Malkiyahu the son of Rekhav, ruler of the district of Beit-Hakerem, repaired the Dung Gate. He rebuilt it and set its doors, its bolts, and its bars.

15 And Shallun the son of Kolhozeh, ruler of the district of Mitzpah, repaired the Fountain Gate. He rebuilt it and covered it and set its doors, its bolts, and its bars. And he built the wall of the Pool of Shelah of the king's garden, as far as the stairs that go down from the city of Dawid.

16 After him Nekhemyah the son of Azvuk, ruler of half the district of Beit-Tzur, repaired to a point opposite the graves of Dawid, as far as the artificial pool, and as far as the house of the mighty men.

17 After him the Levites repaired: Rekhum the son of Bani. Next to him Hashabyahu, ruler of half the district of Ke'ilah, repaired for his district.

18 After him their brothers repaired: Bavvai the son of Henadad, ruler of half the district of Ke'ilah.

19 Next to him Ezer the son of Yeshua, ruler of Mitzpah, repaired another section opposite the ascent to the armory at the Angle.

20 After him Barukh the son of Zabbai repaired another section from the Angle to the door of the house of Elyashiv the high priest.

21 After him Meremot the son of Uriyahu, son of Hakkoz repaired another section from the door of the house of Elyashiv to the end of the house of Elyashiv.

22 After him the priests, the men of the surrounding area, repaired.

23 After them Binyamin and Hashuv repaired opposite their house. After them Azaryahu the son of Ma'aseiyahu, son of Ananyahu repaired beside his own house.

24 After him Binnui the son of Henadad repaired another section, from the house of Azaryahu to the Angle and to the corner.

25 Palal the son of Uzai repaired opposite the Angle and the tower projecting from the upper house of the king at the court of the guard. After him Pedayahu the son of Parosh.

26 And the temple servants living on Ofel repaired to a point opposite the Water Gate on the east and the projecting tower.

27 After him the Tekoites repaired another section opposite the great projecting tower and to the wall of Ofel.

28 Above the Horse Gate the priests repaired, each one opposite his own house.

29 After them Tzadok the son of Immer repaired opposite his own house. After him Shemayahu the son of Shekhaniyahu, the keeper of the East Gate, repaired.

30 After him Hananyahu the son of Shelemyahu, and Hanun the sixth son of Tzalaf, repaired another section. After him Meshullam the son of Berekhyahu repaired opposite his chamber.

31 After him Malkiyahu, one of the goldsmiths, repaired as far as the house of the temple servants and of the merchants, opposite the Muster Gate, and to the upper room of the corner.

32 And between the upper room of the corner and the Sheep Gate the goldsmiths and the merchants repaired.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant register of Chapter 3 is the covenant labor record of the covenant wall-building project: every covenant section, every covenant builder, every covenant family covenant participation documented with covenant precision. The covenant community's covenant willingness — with the notable exception of the Tekoite nobles, verse 5 — is the covenant foundation of the covenant rapid building completion. The covenant text preserves every covenant name because every covenant contribution mattered.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 12 — Shallum the son of Hallohesh, ruler of half the district of Yerushalayim, repaired, he and his daughters. The covenant inclusion of women in the covenant building project: Shallum's daughters are documented as covenant participants in the covenant wall repair. The covenant community's covenant restoration effort is covenant communally inclusive.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 3 in full. The recurring phrase opposite his own house or beside his own house — verses 10, 23, 28, 29 — documents the covenant practical wisdom of the building assignment: the covenant builders are assigned to repair the covenant sections nearest their own homes. Covenant community investment is maximized when the covenant builders protect their own covenant households through covenant communal covenant labor.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5 — the Tekoites repaired, but their nobles would not stoop to serve their lord. The covenant honest documentation of covenant non-participation: within the covenant register of covenant faithful covenant building, the covenant failure of the Tekoite nobles is covenant specifically recorded. The covenant text does not smooth over covenant communal covenant failure — it documents it alongside covenant communal covenant faithfulness. The covenant record preserves both covenant participation and covenant absence.

NEKHEMYAH — CHAPTER 4 Sanballat Mocked the Yehudim — They Will Not Finish — Do Not Be Afraid — Hear O Our Creator — Half Held Spears — With One Hand He Did the Work and With the Other Held His Weapon

1 Now when Sanballat heard that we were building the wall, he was angry and greatly enraged, and he jeered at the Yehudim.

2 And he said in the presence of his brothers and of the army of Samaria, What are these feeble Yehudim doing? Will they restore it for themselves? Will they sacrifice? Will they finish up in a day? Will they revive the stones out of the heaps of rubbish, and burned ones at that?

3 Toviyahu the Ammonite was beside him, and he said, Yes, what they are building — if a fox goes up on it he will break down their stone wall.

4 Hear, O our Creator, for we are despised. Turn back their taunt on their own heads and give them up to be plundered in a land where they are captives.

5 Do not cover their guilt, and let not their sin be blotted out from your sight, for they have provoked you to anger in the presence of the builders.

6 So we built the wall. And all the wall was joined together to half its height, for the people had a mind to work.

7 But when Sanballat and Toviyahu and the Arabs and the Ammonites and the Ashdodites heard that the repairing of the walls of Yerushalayim was going forward and that the breaches were beginning to be closed, they were very angry.

8 And they all plotted together to come and fight against Yerushalayim and to cause confusion in it.

9 And we prayed to our Creator and set a guard as a protection against them day and night.

10 In Yehudah it was said, The strength of those who bear the burdens is failing. There is too much rubble. By ourselves we will not be able to rebuild the wall.

11 And our enemies said, They will not know or see till we come among them and kill them and stop the work.

12 At that time the Yehudim who lived near them came from all directions and said to us ten times, You must return to us.

13 So in the lowest parts of the space behind the wall, in open places, I stationed the people by their clans, with their swords, their spears, and their bows.

14 And I looked and arose and said to the nobles and to the officials and to the rest of the people, Do not be afraid of them. Remember the Creator, who is great and awesome, and fight for your brothers, your sons, your daughters, your wives, and your homes.

15 When our enemies heard that it was known to us and that Creator had frustrated their plan, we all returned to the wall, each to his work.

16 From that day on, half of my servants worked on construction, and half held the spears, shields, bows, and coats of mail. And the leaders stood behind the whole house of Yehudah,

17 Who were building on the wall. Those who carried burdens were loaded in such a way that each labored on the work with one hand and held his weapon with the other.

18 And each of the builders had his sword strapped at his side while he built. The man who sounded the trumpet was beside me.

19 And I said to the nobles and to the officials and to the rest of the people, The work is great and widely spread, and we are separated on the wall, far from one another.

20 In the place where you hear the sound of the trumpet, rally to us there. Our Creator will fight for us.

21 So we labored at the work, and half of them held the spears from the break of dawn until the stars came out.

22 I also said to the people at that time, Let every man and his servant pass the night within Yerushalayim, that they may be a guard for us by night and may labor by day.

23 So neither I nor my brothers nor my servants nor the men of the guard who followed me, none of us took off our clothes. Each kept his weapon at his right hand.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant dual-mode operation of Chapter 4 — half building, half guarding — is the covenant practical expression of the covenant prayer of verse 9: we prayed to our Creator and set a guard. The covenant prayer and the covenant practical preparedness are not alternatives but covenant complements. The covenant community that prays and does not prepare is covenant negligent; the covenant community that prepares and does not pray is covenant self-reliant.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 14 — remember the Creator, who is great and awesome, and fight for your brothers, your sons, your daughters, your wives, and your homes. The covenant double motivation of the covenant defenders: the covenant vertical — remember the Creator — and the covenant horizontal — fight for your families. The covenant community's covenant defense is both covenant theological and covenant relational.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 6 — so we built the wall. And all the wall was joined together to half its height, for the people had a mind to work. The covenant explanation of the covenant rapid covenant progress: the people had a mind — lev — a heart to work. Covenant community covenant project success is covenant heart-driven, not merely covenant labor-driven. The covenant community that is covenant internally covenant motivated achieves covenant results that covenant external covenant supervision cannot produce.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17 — each labored on the work with one hand and held his weapon with the other. The covenant image of the covenant construction-under-threat: the covenant builder who cannot lay down his covenant weapon to work with both hands is working at covenant reduced covenant efficiency. The covenant community accepts covenant reduced covenant efficiency as the covenant cost of covenant security in covenant hostile covenant circumstances. The covenant wall is built — more slowly, more difficultly — but it is built.

NEKHEMYAH — CHAPTER 5 The People Cried Out Against Their Brothers — We Are Mortgaging Our Fields — You Are Exacting Interest — The Thing They Were Doing Was Not Good — I Also Lent Money — Remember Me O My Creator

1 Now there arose a great outcry of the people and of their wives against their Jewish brothers.

2 For there were those who said, With our sons and our daughters, we are many. So let us get grain, that we may eat and keep alive.

3 There were also those who said, We are mortgaging our fields, our vineyards, and our houses to get grain because of the famine.

4 And there were those who said, We have borrowed money for the king's tax on our fields and our vineyards.

5 Now our flesh is as the flesh of our brothers, our children are as their children. Yet we are forcing our sons and our daughters to be slaves, and some of our daughters have already been enslaved, but it is not in our power to help it, for other men have our fields and our vineyards.

6 I was very angry when I heard their outcry and these words.

7 I took counsel with myself, and I brought charges against the nobles and the officials. I said to them, You are exacting interest, each from his brother. And I held a great assembly against them

8 And said to them, We, as far as we are able, have bought back our Jewish brothers who have been sold to the nations, but you even sell your brothers that they may be sold to us. They were silent and could not find a word to say.

9 So I said, The thing that you are doing is not good. Ought you not to walk in the fear of our Creator to prevent the taunts of our enemies the nations?

10 Moreover, I and my brothers and my servants are lending them money and grain. Let us abandon this exacting of interest.

11 Return to them this very day their fields, their vineyards, their olive orchards, and their houses, and the percentage of money, grain, wine, and oil that you have been exacting from them.

12 Then they said, We will restore these and require nothing from them. We will do as you say. And I called the priests and made them swear to do as they had promised.

13 I also shook out the fold of my garment and said, So may Creator shake out every man from his house and from his labor who does not keep this promise. So may he be shaken out and emptied. And all the assembly said Amen and praised YHWH. And the people did as they had promised.

14 Moreover, from the time that I was appointed to be their governor in the land of Yehudah, from the twentieth year to the thirty-second year of Artakhshasta the king, twelve years, neither I nor my brothers ate the food allowance of the governor.

15 The former governors who were before me laid heavy burdens on the people and took from them for their daily ration forty shekels of silver. Even their servants lorded it over the people. But I did not do so, because of the fear of Creator.

16 I also persevered in the work on this wall, and we acquired no land, and all my servants were gathered there for the work.

17 Moreover, there were at my table 150 men, Yehudim and officials, besides those who came to us from the nations that were around us.

18 Now what was prepared at my expense for each day was one ox and six choice sheep and birds, and every ten days all kinds of wine in abundance. Yet for all this I did not demand the food allowance of the governor, because the service was too heavy on this people.

19 Remember for my good, O my Creator, all that I have done for this people.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant internal covenant crisis of Chapter 5 is the covenant most dangerous threat to the covenant restoration project: not the covenant external covenant enemies of Chapters 3-4 but the covenant internal covenant exploitation of the covenant poor by the covenant wealthy within the covenant community itself. The covenant wall-building is threatened not by Sanballat's covenant swords but by the covenant community's covenant economic covenant injustice.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 9 — ought you not to walk in the fear of our Creator to prevent the taunts of our enemies the nations? Nekhemyah's covenant appeal is both covenant theological — fear of Creator — and covenant reputational — the taunts of the nations. The covenant community's covenant internal covenant injustice damages the covenant community's covenant witness before the covenant watching nations.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 14-18 — Nekhemyah's covenant self-disclosure of his own covenant economic covenant practice: twelve years as covenant governor without taking the covenant food allowance — feeding 150 people at his own expense. The covenant leader demonstrates the covenant standard he demands of others. The covenant appeal to covenant economic covenant justice is covenant backed by the covenant leader's covenant personal covenant example.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — I took counsel with myself. The covenant leader's covenant internal covenant deliberation before the covenant public covenant confrontation: Nekhemyah does not immediately react to the covenant injustice with covenant public covenant anger — he thinks first. The covenant self-counsel produces the covenant measured covenant response of calling the covenant assembly and making the covenant argument rather than covenant explosive covenant condemnation.

NEKHEMYAH — CHAPTER 6 Come Let Us Meet Together — They Were Trying to Frighten Us — They Sent an Unsealed Letter — There Were Many Who Pledged Loyalty to Toviyahu — The Wall Was Finished in Fifty-Two Days

1 Now when Sanballat and Toviyahu and Geshem the Arab and the rest of our enemies heard that I had built the wall and that there was no breach left in it — although up to that time I had not set up the doors in the gates —

2 Sanballat and Geshem sent to me, saying, Come and let us meet together at Hakkefirin in the plain of Ono. But they intended to do me harm.

3 And I sent messengers to them, saying, I am doing a great work and I cannot come down. Why should the work stop while I leave it and come down to you?

4 And they sent to me four times in this way, and I answered them in the same manner.

5 In the same way Sanballat for the fifth time sent his servant to me with an open letter in his hand.

6 In it was written, It is reported among the nations, and Geshem also says it, that you and the Yehudim intend to rebel. That is why you are building the wall. And according to these reports you wish to become their king.

7 And you have also set up prophets to proclaim concerning you in Yerushalayim, There is a king in Yehudah. And now the king will hear of these reports. So now come and let us take counsel together.

8 Then I sent to him, saying, No such things as you say have been done, for you are inventing them out of your own mind.

9 For they all wanted to frighten us, thinking, Their hands will drop from the work, and it will not be done. But now, O Creator, strengthen my hands.

10 Now when I went into the house of Shemayahu the son of Delayahu, son of Mehetavel, who was confined to his home, he said, Let us meet together in the house of Creator, within the temple. Let us close the doors of the temple, for they are coming to kill you. They are coming to kill you by night.

11 But I said, Should such a man as I run away? And what man such as I could go into the temple and live? I will not go in.

12 And I understood and saw that Creator had not sent him, but he had pronounced the prophecy against me because Toviyahu and Sanballat had hired him.

13 For this purpose he was hired, that I should be afraid and act in this way and sin, and so they could give me a bad name in order to taunt me.

14 Remember Toviyahu and Sanballat, O my Creator, according to these things that they did, and also the prophetess No'adyahu and the rest of the prophets who wanted to make me afraid.

15 So the wall was finished on the twenty-fifth day of the month Elul, in fifty-two days.

16 And when all our enemies heard of it, all the nations around us were afraid and fell greatly in their own esteem, for they perceived that this work had been accomplished with the help of our Creator.

17 Moreover, in those days the nobles of Yehudah sent many letters to Toviyahu, and Toviyahu's letters came to them.

18 For many in Yehudah were bound by oath to him, because he was the son-in-law of Shekhaniyah the son of Arah, and his son Yehohanan had taken the daughter of Meshullam the son of Berekhyahu as his wife.

19 Also they spoke of his good deeds in my presence and reported my words to him. And Toviyahu sent letters to make me afraid.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant fifty-two day completion of the wall — verse 15 — is the covenant documentary evidence that covenant determination and covenant communal covenant mobilization can accomplish covenant monumental covenant tasks in covenant compressed time. The covenant wall that had lain in ruins for over a century is rebuilt in seven and a half weeks. Covenant community covenant focus produces covenant results that astonish the covenant surrounding nations.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] Chapter 12 is the covenant culmination of the Nekhemyah narrative: the covenant dedication of the covenant wall. The two covenant choirs walking in opposite directions around the covenant wall — verse 31, 38 — converge at the covenant temple — verse 40 — for the covenant great covenant sacrifice of covenant thanksgiving. The covenant wall that was built in covenant opposition, in covenant danger, in covenant labor, is now the covenant processional way for the covenant community's covenant greatest covenant celebration.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full. Verse 43 — the joy of Yerushalayim was heard far away. The covenant sound of the covenant restoration: the covenant community's covenant joy is so great that it is heard beyond the covenant city's boundaries. The covenant community that wept over the covenant covenant Torah — Chapter 8 — and confessed covenant sins — Chapter 9 — and made covenant commitments — Chapter 10 — now fills the covenant restored city with covenant joy that is heard in the covenant distance.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 12 in full. Verse 30 — the priests and the Levites purified themselves, and they purified the people and the gates and the wall. The covenant wall that was built for covenant security is covenant consecrated by covenant purification before the covenant dedication. The covenant civic structure becomes a covenant sacred object through the covenant act of covenant priestly covenant purification.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 43 — Creator had made them rejoice with great joy. The covenant attribution of the covenant joy: the covenant community's covenant rejoicing is covenant attributed to YHWH's covenant action — he made them rejoice. The covenant joy of the covenant dedication is not merely covenant human emotional response to covenant achievement but covenant divine gift given to the covenant community. Covenant joy is the covenant fruit of covenant divine covenant generosity, not merely covenant human covenant satisfaction.

NEKHEMYAH — CHAPTER 13 On That Day They Read — Toviyahu Had Been Given a Large Chamber — I Rebuked the Officials — I Contended With the Nobles Who Were Selling on the Shabbat — I Contended With Them and Cursed Them — Remember Me O My Creator

1 On that day they read from the Book of Mosheh in the hearing of the people. And in it was found written that no Ammonite or Moabite should ever enter the assembly of Creator,

2 For they did not meet the people of Yisra'EL with bread and water, but hired Bil'am against them to curse them — yet our Creator turned the curse into a blessing.

3 As soon as the people heard the Torah, they separated from Yisra'EL all those of foreign descent.

4 Now before this, Elyashiv the priest, who was appointed over the chambers of the house of our Creator, and who was related to Toviyahu,

5 Had prepared for Toviyahu a large chamber where they had previously put the grain offering, the frankincense, the vessels, and the tithes of grain, wine, and oil, which were given by commandment to the Levites, singers, and gatekeepers, and the contributions for the priests.

6 While all this was taking place, I was not in Yerushalayim, for in the thirty-second year of Artakhshasta king of Babylon I had gone to the king. And after some time I asked leave of the king

7 And came to Yerushalayim, and I then discovered the evil that Elyashiv had done for Toviyahu, in preparing for him a chamber in the courts of the house of Creator.

8 And I was very angry, and I threw all the household furniture of Toviyahu out of the chamber.

9 Then I gave orders, and they cleansed the chambers, and I brought back there the vessels of the house of Creator, with the grain offering and the frankincense.

10 I also found out that the portions of the Levites had not been given to them, so that the Levites and the singers, who did the work, had fled each to his field.

11 So I rebuked the officials and said, Why is the house of Creator forsaken? And I gathered them together and set them in their stations.

12 Then all Yehudah brought the tithe of the grain, wine, and oil into the storehouses.

13 And I appointed as treasurers over the storehouses Shelemyahu the priest, Tzadok the scribe, and Pedayahu of the Levites, and as their assistant Hanan the son of Zakkur, son of Mattanyahu, for they were considered reliable, and their duty was to distribute to their brothers.

14 Remember me, O my Creator, concerning this, and do not wipe out my good deeds that I have done for the house of my Creator and for his service.

15 In those days I saw in Yehudah people treading winepresses on the Shabbat, and bringing in heaps of grain and loading them on donkeys, and also wine, grapes, figs, and all kinds of loads, which they brought into Yerushalayim on the Shabbat day. And I warned them on the day when they sold food.

16 Tyrians also, who lived in the city, brought in fish and all kinds of goods and sold them on the Shabbat to the people of Yehudah, in Yerushalayim itself.

17 Then I rebuked the nobles of Yehudah and said to them, What is this evil thing that you are doing, profaning the Shabbat day?

18 Did not your fathers act in this way, and did not our Creator bring all this disaster on us and on this city? Now you are bringing more wrath on Yisra'EL by profaning the Shabbat.

19 As soon as it began to grow dark at the gates of Yerushalayim before the Shabbat, I commanded that the doors should be shut and gave orders that they should not be opened until after the Shabbat. And I stationed some of my servants at the gates, so that no load might be brought in on the Shabbat day.

20 Then the merchants and sellers of all kinds of wares spent the night outside Yerushalayim once or twice.

21 But I warned them and said to them, Why do you spend the night outside the wall? If you do so again, I will lay hands on you. From that time on they did not come on the Shabbat.

22 Then I commanded the Levites that they should purify themselves and come and guard the gates, to keep the Shabbat day holy. Remember this also in my favor, O my Creator, and spare me according to the greatness of your steadfast love.

23 In those days also I saw the Yehudim who had married women of Ashdod, Ammon, and Moav.

24 And half of their children spoke the language of Ashdod, and they could not speak the language of Yehudah, but only the language of each people.

25 And I contended with them and cursed them and beat some of them and pulled out their hair. And I made them take an oath in the name of Creator, saying, You shall not give your daughters to their sons, or take their daughters for your sons or for yourselves.

26 Did not Shelomoh king of Yisra'EL sin on account of such women? Among the many nations there was no king like him, and he was beloved by his Creator, and Creator made him king over all Yisra'EL. Nevertheless, foreign women made even him to sin.

27 Shall we then listen to you and do all this great evil and act treacherously against our Creator by marrying foreign women?

28 And one of the sons of Yoyada, the son of Elyashiv the high priest, was the son-in-law of Sanballat the Horonite. Therefore I chased him from me.

29 Remember them, O my Creator, because they have desecrated the priesthood and the covenant of the priesthood and the Levites.

30 Thus I cleansed them from everything foreign, and I established the duties of the priests and Levites, each in his work,

31 And I provided for the wood offering at appointed times, and for the firstfruits. Remember me, O my Creator, for good.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] Chapter 13 is the covenant final chapter of the covenant canonical Hebrew historical narrative: Nekhemyah's covenant return after an absence — verse 6 — to find the covenant commitments of Chapter 10 already being violated. The covenant pattern of covenant reform and covenant relapse is the honest covenant conclusion of the covenant historical narrative. The covenant book ends not with covenant triumph but with covenant repeated covenant reform and covenant personal covenant prayer: remember me, O my Creator, for good.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full. The four covenant petitions of Nekhemyah — remember me — verse 14, 22, 29, 31 — are the covenant structure of the final chapter. The covenant governor does not plead his own covenant righteousness but simply asks YHWH to covenant remember his covenant service. The covenant leader's covenant ultimate covenant appeal is not covenant achievement but covenant divine covenant attention.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 13 in full. Verse 24 — half of their children spoke the language of Ashdod, and they could not speak the language of Yehudah. The covenant language of the covenant community is the covenant identity marker: covenant linguistic assimilation — the children cannot speak Yehudi — is the covenant measure of covenant communal covenant erosion. When the covenant community's covenant children cannot speak the covenant language, the covenant identity is being covenant lost at the next covenant generation.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 31 — remember me, O my Creator, for good. The covenant final words of the covenant canonical historical narrative. The covenant governor who rebuilt the covenant walls, covenant reformed the covenant community, and covenant confronted covenant internal covenant violations ends with a covenant personal covenant petition to YHWH. The covenant book that began with Nekhemyah's covenant prayer in Shushan — Chapter 1 — ends with Nekhemyah's covenant prayer in Yerushalayim. Covenant prayer frames the covenant entire narrative.
